headline.text: L1 S2 bark breaker coating *chipping* into contact zone — equipment finish degrading, hold and repair needed before restart.

summary: One active hold at L3 ML4 (5 partial rolls, quality issue from 3rd shift). Three direct food-safety flags: bark breaker coating flaking at L1 S2, vanilla dosing fault at L1 S4 (caught before impact), and paper towels over product zone at L3 Truffle.

holds.title: 5 partial rolls held — quality defect
holds.summary: 3rd shift left 5 partial rolls marked as bad; 1st shift verified defects and placed on hold at 11:11 MT.

food_safety.title: Bark breaker coating chipping off
food_safety.summary: Charity Young documented finish degradation on S2 bark breaker food-contact surface — coating flaking risk into product flow.
food_safety.status: Victor Romero Sr. scheduled reclean for following shift; equipment needs inspection and repair before use.

food_safety.title: S4 vanilla pump dosing fault
food_safety.summary: Pump configuration jumped from 220 g/h to 12,000 g/h mid-batch; operator caught, stopped pump immediately, maintenance reset system.
food_safety.status: Resolved — pump reset, tested, and verified normal operation by 10:49 MT; QA sensory tested batch and confirmed no product impact.

food_safety.title: S4 C2 caramel overfilled into steam pipe
food_safety.summary: C2 overfilled to the point caramel pushed up the steam pipe; unclear if pressure forced it or tank overfilled.
food_safety.status: CRU production paused; C1 batch finished, C2 caramel scrapped; investigating root cause.

food_safety.title: Paper towels covering pipes above Sweco
food_safety.summary: Angel Cabrera flagged paper towels used as covers over pipes directly above product sifter — foreign material and fiber risk.
food_safety.status: Open; Angel requested caps instead of towels, awaiting maintenance response.

quality.title: 6 pallets Tate's wrong lot code printed
quality.summary: S2 first shift printed incorrect lot number on 6 pallets; flagged to 2nd shift supervisor for rework.
quality.status: Rework verified and started by 2nd shift QA at 15:49 MT.

quality.title: Chocolate pallets expired labels but extended shelf life
quality.summary: S2 shift found chocolate BBD labels showing expiration before receipt date; NetSuite shows shelf life extended to 7/10/26 but labels not updated.
quality.status: Open — IMS contacted, labels require update to reflect extended BBD; received date also incorrect.

quality.title: 10 pallets S2 jars oriented incorrectly
quality.summary: Final inspection found jar labels not forward-facing per customer spec; wrapper operator repeatedly instructed but non-compliance continued.
quality.status: Resolved — all 10 pallets corrected before shift end at 06:31 MT.

sanitation.title: ML4 TG4 vacuum system failure
sanitation.summary: 2nd shift operator noticed TG4 vacuum not working; bins suggest it may have been down all day, dust not evacuating.
sanitation.status: Resolved — maintenance cleared clogged funnel filter by 17:08 MT, vacuum restored.

sanitation.title: ML4 TG5 main vacuum filter clogged
sanitation.summary: TG5 multiples issue traced to main vacuum filter (not CIL-cleaned); filter clogged, causing secondary funnel blockage.
sanitation.status: Resolved — filter cleaned, TG5 running normally; Shawn Jewkes working on daily cleaning standard and schedule.

opportunities.text: Consider pre-shift food-contact surface inspection for S2 bark breaker and similar coated equipment — today's chipping finish was visible and should have been caught before product contact.
Review label verification and operator accountability on S2 pack-off — 10 pallets required rework for orientation non-compliance despite repeated instruction, signaling a training or supervision gap.
Add TG vacuum filter cleaning to daily or per-shift maintenance checklist — today's TG4 and TG5 clogs caused line issues and suggest the current CIL scope misses critical dust-handling components.
Replace temporary paper-towel covers over Truffle Sweco with permanent caps — today's flag highlights a recurring foreign-material risk that a simple engineering control would eliminate.
